humanities_role_explanation: Evaluates both domain-specific historical content knowledge and specialized methodological workflows of historical research, such as paleography, interdisciplinary synthesis, and primary source processing.
benchmark_modalities_explanation: A multimodal setup combining textual questions with visual sources (manuscripts, maps, illustrations, inscriptions), audio recordings (oral history), and historical video clips.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Evaluates complex historical inquiry skills, including factual retrieval from primary sources, processing non-textual or damaged formats, causal reasoning, interdisciplinary synthesis (e.g., archaeology and linguistics), and decoding cultural contexts.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Specifically designed as a domain-specific evaluation suite targeting historical reasoning and historical research workflows.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates LLMs including GPT-4o, DeepSeek-R1, Grok 3, o4-mini-high, and o3.

llm_is_primary_system.note: Generative LLMs and LLM-based agent systems are the primary systems evaluated.

arts_humanities_data.note: Includes data from History, Classics, Archaeology, Language and Linguistics, Religious Studies, and Visual Arts and Performing Arts.

quantitative_metrics.note: Evaluates using pass@1 and pass@2 accuracy metrics.

published_after_march_2023.note: Preprint published on arXiv in June 2025.

english_language.note: Paper and metadata are in English, with primary source benchmark materials spanning 29 languages.

summary: This paper introduces HistBench, a multimodal, multilingual benchmark of 414 expert-curated questions designed to assess historical reasoning. To overcome the limitations of generalist LLMs on these tasks, the authors propose HistAgent, a domain-specialized framework equipped with tools for historical OCR, translation, and academic literature search.
key_findings: State-of-the-art models equipped with generic web search struggle on HistBench (e.g., GPT-4o achieves 18.60% pass@1). The custom HistAgent framework, built on GPT-4o, significantly outperforms these baselines, reaching 27.54% pass@1 and 36.47% pass@2, while maintaining strong performance on general benchmarks like GAIA.
review_notes: Minor disagreements regarding the exact lists of analytical tasks and ASJC areas. Resolved analytical tasks by strictly adopting the six formal evaluation dimensions defined in Table 1 of the paper. Resolved ASJC areas by including domains explicitly supported by the text (History, Classics, Archaeology, Linguistics, Religious Studies, Visual Arts) and omitting Philosophy, which was not explicitly highlighted as a core dataset domain despite some author affiliations.
paper_name: On Path to Multimodal Historical Reasoning: HistBench and HistAgent
